name: Zámek Lednice
hook: Novogotický zámek Lichtenštejnů uprostřed krajiny UNESCO.
blurb: Letní sídlo Lichtenštejnů, v polovině 19. století přestavěné do romantické novogotiky po vzoru anglických tudorovských sídel. Uvnitř jsou bohatě vyřezávané dřevěné interiéry — slavné točité schodiště v knihovně je vyřezáno z jediného dubu — a hned vedle stojí palmový skleník z roku 1845, jeden z nejstarších litinových skleníků v Evropě. Zámek je srdcem Lednicko-valtického areálu, komponované krajiny zámků, rybníků a romantických staveb, která je od roku 1996 na seznamu UNESCO.
admission: okruhy od cca 250 Kč · park zdarma

name: Minaret (Lednice)
hook: 302 schodů na jediný minaret v Česku — panorama celého parku.
blurb: Šedesátimetrová romantická rozhledna, kterou si na přelomu 18. a 19. století nechali postavit Lichtenštejnové podle návrhu Josefa Hardtmutha — architekta a mimochodem zakladatele tužkárny Koh-i-noor. Patří k nejstarším dochovaným rozhlednám v českých zemích. Tři ochozy nabízejí výhled na Zámecký rybník se zarostlými ostrůvky, celý zámecký park a za dobré viditelnosti až na Pálavu.

name: Lednické rybníky (Lednice Ponds)
hook: Wildlife-rich pond chain with wooded islands behind the chateau.
blurb: The Zámecký rybník behind Lednice chateau is dotted with 15 artificial islands and forms the northern end of the Lednice ponds, a Ramsar-listed wetland and one of Moravia's most important bird areas. Great for herons, cormorants and waterfowl from the park paths.

name: Janův hrad (Janohrad / John's Castle)
hook: A deliberately-built mock medieval ruin in the floodplain forest.
blurb: An early-19th-century artificial 'ruined castle' erected as a romantic hunting lodge and eye-catcher in the Dyje floodplain woods east of Lednice. Its four crumbling-look towers around a courtyard are genuinely habitable inside — an atmospheric woodland stop about 2 km from the chateau.

name: Chrám Tří grácií (Temple of the Three Graces)
hook: Elegant colonnade folly with a Three Graces sculpture on the pond shore.
blurb: A neoclassical horseshoe colonnade of twelve Ionic columns built in 1824–25 on the south bank of the Prostřední rybník. In front stands Fischer's 1799 sculpture of the Three Graces — one of the loveliest follies scattered through the Lednice–Valtice landscape.

name: Státní zámek Valtice (Valtice Chateau)
hook: Grand Baroque Liechtenstein seat, with the national wine cellars beneath.
blurb: One of the finest Baroque residences in the country and former principal seat of the Liechtensteins, with richly decorated state rooms and a chapel. Its cellars house the Czech National Wine Salon, where you can taste the country's top 100 wines.

name: Kolonáda na Rajstně / Reistna
hook: Hilltop colonnade at the estate's highest point, with views to Austria.
blurb: A classicist 24-column colonnade built 1817–23 as a belvedere on Homole hill (280 m), the loftiest spot in the Lednice–Valtice area, right on the Austrian border. On a clear day it sees Mikulov, the Pálava hills, the great Nesyt pond and even the Little Carpathians in Slovakia.

name: Coffeesquare Lednice
hook: Specialty-coffee roastery and cafe steps from the chateau.
blurb: A modern cafe opened in 2019 inside its own roastery on Valtická street, just off the centre of Lednice. Freshly roasted specialty coffee, filter tastings, homemade desserts and local wines — the standout coffee stop at the western end of the route.

name: Café Hostina (Břeclav)
hook: Specialty espresso and cakes with a leafy plane-tree garden.
blurb: A Břeclav cafe praised for carefully prepared espresso and filter coffee, homemade cakes and a pretty garden shaded by plane trees. A convenient quality caffeine stop right at the Day 1 start.

name: Rozhledna Kraví hora u Bořetic
hook: Wooden tower over a sea of vineyards — the quirky 'Kraví hora Republic'.
blurb: A 15m timber lookout built in 2006 on Olbramy hill, with a viewing gallery reached by 65 steps. It surveys the patchwork of wine-growing hills, the Pálava range and the Nové Mlýny reservoirs, amid the tongue-in-cheek 'Free Federal Republic of Kraví hora' wine-cellar colony.

name: Zámek Čejkovice a Templářské sklepy
hook: Fortress-chateau over vast 13th-century Templar wine cellars.
blurb: Founded by the Knights Templar in the 13th century, this fortified chateau preserves extensive Gothic underground vaults now used as the Templářské sklepy winery, among the oldest wine cellars in the region. The young Jan Amos Komenský (Comenius) lived here as a boy.

name: Vyhlídka Soudek (Barrel Lookout)
hook: Wooden barrel-shaped lookout in the vineyards — a classic Toskánsko vantage.
blurb: A 4 m wooden mini-tower shaped like a wine barrel, built in 2017 in the Požární čtvrtě vineyard tract at 252 m. From the platform you look over rolling vineyards and wavy fields toward Babí lom, Chřiby with Buchlov castle, the White Carpathians, Pálava and even Milotice chateau. Free, year-round; the Mutěnice wine trail passes by.

name: Rozhledna Vyšicko
hook: 12 m triangular wooden tower on the hill above the Mutěnice cellars.
blurb: Opened in 2018 on Vyšicko hill, the 12 m timber-and-steel tower has a triangular plan evoking a grape bunch, with a roofed platform reached by 46 steps. It overlooks Mutěnice's fields and vineyards, with Pálava, the White Carpathians, Babí lom and Chřiby on the horizon. Free, open all year, parking at the base.

name: Rozhledna Súsedská
hook: 21 m tower above Podchřibí — sweeping Kyjovsko panorama.
blurb: Completed in 2021 as a joint project of Hýsly, Ježov and Žádovice, this 21 m concrete-and-steel tower (99 steps) gives a wide sweep over rolling Kyjovsko, Pálava, the Buchlov hills, Chřiby, the Ždánice forest and, on clear days, Slovakia and Austria. Reach it via the Kyjovská cycle path.

name: Kaplička sv. Barborky (lone chapel in the fields)
hook: The lone white baroque chapel in the fields — arguably THE icon of Moravian Tuscany.
blurb: A small baroque chapel standing utterly alone in endless fields below Nová hora at about 250 m, near Strážovice. One of the single most photographed subjects in the whole region — best with a long lens from the photographers' vantage so power lines stay out of frame. Magical in spring green and golden autumn mornings. ⚠︎ Coordinates approximate.

name: Babí lom (Strážovský vrch)
hook: At 417 m the highest point of the Kyjov uplands — the landscape spreads out below.
blurb: The mostly wooded summit above Strážovice (marked by a radio transmitter) is one of the region's premier overlooks, the wavy farmland unfolding to the south, views reaching the White Carpathians and occasionally the Alps. A bench sits by memorial lindens. Reached by asphalt road or green-marked trail. ⚠︎ Coordinates approximate.

name: Karlínské kaštany (Karlín Chestnut Avenue)
hook: A photogenic line of chestnut trees marching over the wavy fields.
blurb: One of the named 'fotomísta' of Moravian Tuscany: a row of chestnut trees (and the nearby 'Karlínské vlnky' rolling folds) that photographers use to lead the eye across the corrugated farmland. Best at low sun, morning or evening, when the ridges throw long shadows. ⚠︎ Approximate — it's a stretch of field track, not a fixed monument.

name: Posedy u Stavěšic (lone hunting perches)
hook: Lone wooden hunting hides on bare ridgelines — a signature Toskánsko silhouette.
blurb: The wooden 'posedy' standing solitary on the wavy ridges around Stavěšice have become icons of the area, a single graphic subject against the striped fields. They work beautifully as silhouette/foreground at sunrise and sunset. ⚠︎ Exact position varies; scout the field tracks north of the village.

name: Šardické biopásy (contour crop strips)
hook: Contour-planted crop strips that turn the hills into a striped tapestry.
blurb: Around Šardice the fields are farmed in narrow contour bands (biopásy) plus a small remízek grove, producing the layered, multi-colour striping that makes this the 'Moravian Tuscany' look. Combine with the Soudek lookout nearby. Colours peak with spring rapeseed yellow and fresh green. ⚠︎ Coordinates approximate.

name: Výhled nad Sobůlky (vineyard overlook)
hook: Vineyard slopes and cellars looking back over the Kyjov basin.
blurb: Sobůlky sits amid vineyard-covered slopes on the approach to Babí lom, giving open views back across the Kyjov basin and the wavy fields. Quieter and less-photographed than Soudek, good for evening light over the vine rows and cellar lanes. ⚠︎ Position approximate along the vineyard tracks.

name: Státní zámek Milotice
hook: The 'pearl of southeast Moravia' — a superbly preserved baroque chateau & French garden.
blurb: One of the most completely preserved baroque building-and-garden complexes in the country, given its high-baroque form under Karel Antonín Serényi in 1719–1743. The 4.5 ha French formal garden (from 1716) is laid out with geometric precision and merges into the surrounding flat landscape. A marquee stop and frequent film location.

name: Zámek Ždánice a Vrbasovo muzeum
hook: Renaissance-core, baroque four-wing chateau with a quirky regional museum.
blurb: A four-wing chateau with a Renaissance core (built for Oldřich z Kounic in the 1560s) and baroque remodelling under the Liechtensteins, with corner prism towers. Its south wing has housed the Vrbas Museum since 1946 — some 6,000 exhibits including eight large movable models of a historic village. At the edge of the Ždánice forest.

name: Kostel sv. Anny (Žarošice pilgrimage church)
hook: Major Marian pilgrimage church — the 'Old Mother of God' and Golden Saturday.
blurb: The parish church (first recorded 1326, rebuilt 1800–1801 after the 1797 fire) is one of Moravia's notable Marian pilgrimage sites, holding the statue of the Old Mother of God moved here in 1785. Its biggest pilgrimage, 'Zlatá sobota' (Golden Saturday), falls on the Saturday after 8 September.

name: Kyjovská radnice (Renaissance town hall)
hook: Ornate 1560s Renaissance town hall with a 39 m tower on the main square.
blurb: Built in 1561–1562 with the tower raised in 1582, the richly sgraffito-decorated town hall is the centrepiece of Kyjov's Masarykovo náměstí and a protected monument. A natural base for the region and a good coffee/rest stop, with the town museum and services around the square.

name: Pražírna Kyjov (roastery café)
hook: Specialty roastery-café roasting its own single-origin (ZRNA) coffee.
blurb: A stylish roastery and café near the centre of Kyjov, open since 2015, serving freshly roasted specialty coffee under its own ZRNA brand plus homemade desserts. Doubles as a cultural space for concerts and exhibitions. The most specialty-focused option in town.

name: Amazonka — kavárna & bistro
hook: Specialty-coffee bistro on the main square, listed on European Coffee Trip.
blurb: A café-bistro on Kyjov's Masarykovo náměstí serving rotating specialty coffee — a fruity espresso and a Brazil daily plus two filters — alongside brunches, breakfasts, homemade lemonades and cocktails. A friendly modern cyclist refuel.

name: Kavárna & cukrárna Longus
hook: Traditional Viennese-style café-patisserie, everything made in-house.
blurb: A café and cukrárna in the heart of Kyjov serving pastries, cakes and ice cream all produced in its own workshop, with the atmosphere of a classic Viennese coffee house. The cake-forward counterpart to the specialty spots — good for a sweet stop on the square.

name: Hrad Buchlov (Buchlov Castle)
hook: Mighty royal Gothic castle crowning the Chřiby ridge.
blurb: One of the oldest and best-preserved royal castles in Moravia, founded in the first half of the 13th century on a wooded hilltop above Buchlovice. Never conquered, it houses museum collections, an armoury and a famous hunting-trophy hall. The keep offers sweeping views over the Chřiby forests.

name: Zámek Buchlovice
hook: Italian-style Baroque chateau with romantic gardens.
blurb: An elegant Baroque summer residence built around 1700 for the Petřvaldský family in the style of an Italian villa, with two wings around an oval courtyard and fountain. It's surrounded by a large English park famous for its fuchsias and old trees, just below Buchlov Castle — the two forming a classic pair.

name: Kaple sv. Barbory / Barborka
hook: Baroque hilltop chapel on a supposed pagan sacrificial site.
blurb: A striking early-Baroque chapel from 1672–1673 on a Greek-cross plan atop Modla hill, a short walk from Buchlov Castle. It served as the burial chapel of the Buchlov lords and a pilgrimage site, reputedly on an ancient pagan cult place. The clearing gives fine views across the Chřiby.

name: Rozhledna Brdo
hook: Stone tower on Brdo, the highest peak of the Chřiby.
blurb: A 24 m stone lookout opened in 2004 on Brdo (587 m), the highest point of the Chřiby. The 99-step climb rewards you with views to the Beskydy, White Carpathians and Pálava. ⚠︎ Roughly 7 km north of the corridor — a worthwhile ridge detour.

name: Králův stůl (King's Table)
hook: Mysterious carved sandstone slab deep in the forest.
blurb: A large flat sandstone block (2.6 m) in the woods on the old road from Modrá toward Bunč, first mentioned in a 1228 Velehrad monastery charter as a boundary marker. Legend links it to a meeting of five kings; some read its carved symbols as a prehistoric solar calendar. ⚠︎ ~4 km north of Modrá, a forest detour.

name: Bazilika Velehrad (Velehrad Basilica)
hook: The spiritual heart of Moravia and a great pilgrimage basilica.
blurb: A monumental Baroque basilica on the site of a 13th-century Cistercian monastery, the most important Cyril-and-Methodius pilgrimage site in the country. Each July it draws tens of thousands for the National Pilgrimage; Pope John Paul II visited in 1990. The natural overnight anchor of the route.

name: Archeoskanzen Modrá (Great Moravian village)
hook: Reconstructed 9th-century Great Moravian church and fortified village.
blurb: Beside the excavated foundations of a 9th-century Great Moravian church stands a full-scale reconstruction, next to an open-air museum recreating a fortified Slavic settlement of the Great Moravian era. Opened in 2004, it vividly shows dwellings, palisades and daily life. Just north of Velehrad.

name: Živá voda Modrá
hook: Walk-through glass tunnel beneath a freshwater lake.
blurb: A nature exhibition next to the Modrá archaeological park where an underwater glass tunnel lets you watch sturgeon, catfish and other Morava-river fish swim overhead. Above ground: meadows, ponds and a botanical trail. A relaxing family-friendly stop right by Velehrad.

name: Památník Velké Moravy (Great Moravia Memorial)
hook: Modern museum built over an excavated Great Moravian church.
blurb: A National Cultural Monument in Staré Město, built directly over the foundations of the 'Na Valách' church, one of the key sacral buildings of the 9th-century Great Moravian centre. Exhibits cover the empire's rise, the Cyril-and-Methodius mission and rich archaeological finds. The historic core of Veligrad, forerunner of Uherské Hradiště.

name: Výšina sv. Metoděje (Sady)
hook: Hilltop cradle of the Cyril-and-Methodius mission with a view.
blurb: On a low hill on the SE edge of Uherské Hradiště lie the marked foundations of a 9th-century Great Moravian church-and-power complex, thought by some to be linked to Archbishop Methodius himself. A quiet, atmospheric site with an open outlook over the Morava valley. ⚠︎ Coordinates approximate.

name: Rozhledna Rovnina (Rochus)
hook: Steel tower above town with a 360° Slovácko panorama.
blurb: A 49.5 m tower on Rovnina hill above Mařatice, with a platform at 336 m reached by 125 steps, freely accessible April–October. Views take in Uherské Hradiště, the Morava valley, the Chřiby to the west and the White Carpathians to the east. Adjoins the Park Rochus open-air area.

name: Ostrožská jezera (Slovácké moře)
hook: Chain of turquoise gravel lakes nicknamed the 'Moravian Sea'.
blurb: A large complex of former gravel-pit lakes south of Uherské Hradiště, popular for swimming, diving and fishing; the Albatros natural swimming area is the designated bathing spot. Clear water and reed-fringed shores make a refreshing flat-terrain break. Note: most lakes are a drinking-water source and off-limits for bathing.

name: Zelený pokoj (café & bakery)
hook: Specialty coffee, brunch and fresh croissants off the main square.
blurb: A small café, bistro and bakery in a passage just off Masarykovo náměstí, serving rotating specialty coffees from mostly Czech roasters alongside a brunch menu and fresh croissants. Repeatedly named among the best coffee spots in town — a strong candidate for the best cup of the trip.

name: Café Colejní
hook: Community café in the courtyard of the old Jesuit college.
blurb: A relaxed community café set in the historic courtyard of the former Jesuit college on Masarykovo náměstí, with a garden patio and good coffee in quiet, attractive surroundings. A pleasant, family-friendly stop before rolling on toward Kunovice.

name: Luhačovická přehrada (reservoir)
hook: Forest-ringed dam lake just above the spa town.
blurb: A dam reservoir on the Horní Olšava built in 1930, ringed by wooded hills of the Vizovice highlands. A popular summer spot for swimming, pedal boats and lakeside walks, with a shoreline café near the dam. The gentle 4 km loop around the water is a natural rest point.

name: Rozhledna Velký Lopeník
hook: 22 m oak tower on the White Carpathians' second-highest peak.
blurb: A 22 m wooden lookout opened in 2005 atop Velký Lopeník (911 m), the second-highest summit of the White Carpathians, right on the Czech–Slovak border. On clear days the view reaches across the Bílé Karpaty toward the Chřiby and Javorníky. ⚠︎ A big climb in the far-southern arc — check the detour distance.

name: Rozhledna Doubrava (u Vizovic)
hook: 55 m mast with a viewing gallery over the Vizovice hills.
blurb: A 55 m telecom tower on Doubrava hill (672 m) between Vizovice and Loučka, with a public platform 33 m up (174 steps). The panorama takes in the Vizovické and Hostýnské hills, Zlín, and distant Hostýn, Radhošť and Velká Javořina. Just north of the Vizovice end of the route.

name: Komonec (Vizovické vrchy)
hook: Highest point of the Luhačovické Zálesí, deep beech forest.
blurb: At 672 m, Komonec is the highest hill of the Luhačovické Zálesí, cloaked in beech woods above Podhradí. Marked trails climb past the Starý Světlov castle ruins toward the summit; the ridge is forested rather than a bare overlook, but the shaded tracks are prime bikepacking terrain. ⚠︎ Coordinates approximate.

name: Jurkovičův dům (Jurkovič House)
hook: Luhačovice's iconic Art Nouveau folk-style spa house.
blurb: Built in 1901–02 by Dušan Jurkovič, this is the signature building of Luhačovice spa, blending Art Nouveau with Moravian-Slovak folk motifs in carved timber and colourful gables. Jurkovič reshaped much of the spa's look, making the town a showcase of his 'folk secese' style. Now a hotel at the heart of the colonnade.

name: Lázeňská kolonáda & Vincentka
hook: Free-flowing salty mineral spring in the spa heart.
blurb: The colonnade around Lázeňské náměstí is the social core of Luhačovice, where guests sip from the famous alkaline-saline Vincentka spring, prized for respiratory cures. The surrounding ensemble of spa houses — many in Jurkovič's folk-Art-Nouveau idiom — makes this the fourth-largest spa in Czechia.

name: Zámek Vizovice
hook: Baroque chateau with a French garden in the town centre.
blurb: A Baroque manor built in the 1760s on the site of a former Cistercian monastery, set in a formal French garden in the middle of Vizovice. Interiors preserve period furnishings and a notable painting collection, and the estate hosts concerts and events. Marks the northern turn of the route.

name: Zámek Nový Světlov (Bojkovice)
hook: Neo-Gothic castle on a wooded hill above Bojkovice.
blurb: Originally a 15th-century fortress rebuilt in Romantic neo-Gothic style in the mid-1800s, Nový Světlov crowns a hill over Bojkovice at the edge of the White Carpathians. Now a hotel and event venue, but the turreted exterior and grounds are a striking landmark on the corridor's southern leg.

name: Poutní kostel Maleniska (Provodov)
hook: Forest pilgrimage church with a holy spring.
blurb: A Baroque pilgrimage church of Our Lady of the Snows tucked in the woods at Maleniska near Provodov, built in the 18th century around a revered healing spring. It remains one of eastern Moravia's active Marian pilgrimage sites — quiet and atmospheric among the trees. A short detour north of the reservoir.

name: Muzeum J. A. Komenského (Uh. Brod)
hook: National museum to the 'Teacher of Nations'.
blurb: Uherský Brod's museum honours Jan Amos Komenský (Comenius), the 17th-century educator tied to this region, with exhibits on his life and pedagogy plus local history. A bronze Komenský statue by Vincenc Makovský marks the entrance. The town's Renaissance square and gates are worth a loop.

name: Rodný dům Komenského (Nivnice)
hook: Most likely birthplace of Comenius.
blurb: Nivnice is considered the most probable birthplace of Jan Amos Komenský, traditionally linked to Bartkův mlýn (house No. 99), which bears a 1942 memorial plaque, with a Komenský monument in the village. A modest but meaningful stop on the western part of the corridor. ⚠︎ Coordinates are the village centre.

name: RUDOLF JELÍNEK (slivovice distillery)
hook: Home of Moravian plum brandy (slivovice).
blurb: The Rudolf Jelínek distillery in Vizovice is the most famous producer of Moravian slivovice, distilling here since the early 20th century. Its visitor centre offers tours of the distilling museum, maturation cellars and a tasting room. A fitting, spirited finish near the Vizovice chateau.

name: Mercii Caffè
hook: Specialty coffee in the spa, listed on European Coffee Trip.
blurb: A modern Slovácko-Wallachian café between the colonnade and the dam in Luhačovice, serving specialty coffee alongside homemade pastries and honest Moravian wines. Featured on European Coffee Trip and a reliable specialty stop in a spa town otherwise heavy on traditional cukrárny.

name: JAMAI CAFE
hook: Local roastery café, coffee roasted in town since 2014.
blurb: A family specialty-coffee roastery founded in Uherský Brod in 2014, with cafés on the town's central squares serving daily fresh-roasted coffee, loose teas and homemade lemonades. Its beans even supply the lakeside café at the Luhačovice dam. The strongest specialty pick on the western leg.

name: Baťův mrakodrap – Budova 21
hook: 77 m functionalist skyscraper with Baťa's legendary moving elevator-office.
blurb: Built 1936–1938 by architect Vladimír Karfík, this 16-storey, 77.5 m tower was one of Europe's first skyscrapers. Its most famous feature is the boss's fully mobile air-conditioned office inside a 6×6 m elevator, letting management descend to any factory floor. Today it houses regional offices and a rooftop café with sweeping views.

name: 14|15 Baťův institut
hook: Two converted Baťa factory halls turned cultural district.
blurb: Former shoe-factory buildings 14 and 15, designed by Jiří Voženílek and rebuilt 1946–1949, were converted in 2013 into Zlín's flagship culture campus — the Museum of South-East Moravia, the Regional Gallery of Fine Arts and the regional library, linked by an open-air platform. The architecture gallery displays models of Zlín's functionalist buildings.

name: Památník Tomáše Bati
hook: Glass-and-steel functionalist shrine to the company founder.
blurb: Designed by František Lydie Gahura and built in 1933 after Tomáš Baťa died in a plane crash, this pure functionalist pavilion of glass, steel and concrete originally displayed the wreckage of his aircraft. Its light-filled, column-free interior made it a landmark of interwar Czech modernism. Restored to its 1930s look, it serves as a memorial and concert space.

name: Hrad Malenovice
hook: 14th-century Gothic castle with a climbable tower over the Dřevnice valley.
blurb: Founded around 1356 by Margrave John Henry of Moravia, Malenovice was rebuilt into a Renaissance residence in the 16th century. Since 1953 it has belonged to the regional museum with archaeological, ethnographic and hunting exhibitions. Climb the tower for views across the western approach to Zlín.

name: Zámek Lešná
hook: Neo-Renaissance/Tudor chateau at the heart of the zoo park.
blurb: The romantic chateau was built 1887–1894 for Count František Seilern. Its richly furnished interiors — coffered ceilings, Renaissance-revival halls and period collections — are open for guided tours. It sits inside the grounds of Zlín's celebrated zoo, about 10 km from the city centre. ⚠︎ Check detour distance.

name: Zámek Napajedla
hook: Baroque chateau by the workshop of Fischer von Erlach.
blurb: This Baroque chateau, attributed to the workshop of Johann Bernhard Fischer von Erlach, anchors the small town of Napajedla just north of Otrokovice. Rebuilt and extended over the 18th–19th centuries, it's surrounded by an English-style landscape park overlooking the Morava valley — near the very end of the route.

name: Hřebčín Napajedla (stud farm)
hook: Historic English-thoroughbred stud farm founded 1886.
blurb: Founded in 1886 by Aristide Baltazzi, the Napajedla stud became one of Central Europe's most renowned breeders of English thoroughbred racehorses. Its elegant stables and paddocks sit just below the chateau. A distinctive equestrian landmark on the way into Otrokovice.

name: Zámek Zlín (Hradisko)
hook: Renaissance-Baroque chateau in the city-centre park.
blurb: Originally a Gothic fortress, Zlín's chateau was rebuilt in Renaissance and later Baroque styles and stands in a small park just off the central square. It has served the town's cultural life and today holds exhibition and event spaces — a quiet historic counterpoint to the surrounding Baťa architecture.

name: ZOO Zlín-Lešná
hook: One of Czechia's most-visited zoos, in a chateau park.
blurb: Spread across a landscaped 50+ hectare park around Lešná Chateau, the zoo is organised by continents and famous for its walk-through Yucatan pavilion and African safari expositions. Consistently among the two or three most-visited zoos in the country. A relaxed nature-and-family stop ~10 km NE of central Zlín. ⚠︎ Check detour distance.

name: Lesní hřbitov (Forest Cemetery)
hook: Baťa-era woodland cemetery inspired by North America.
blurb: Conceived by Tomáš Baťa after seeing forest cemeteries in North America and laid out by František Lydie Gahura, this hilltop burial ground blends graves into mixed forest with a single central path. Many Baťa-era figures rest here in a deliberately tranquil setting. Doubles as a peaceful woodland walk on the Kudlov ridge.

name: Park Komenského
hook: Award-winning central chill-out park.
blurb: A compact, well-kept park in the middle of Zlín beside the former Bartoš library, with a modern fountain, benches and a popular playground. Recognised as a 'park of the quarter-century' and a natural green pause between the Baťa buildings — handy for a coffee-in-the-park stop.

name: Vyhlídka nad Kudlovem
hook: Meadow overlook toward Zlín and the Tlustá hora transmitter.
blurb: Reached on the yellow trail descending from the Kudlov roundabout toward Zlín, this meadow viewpoint opens views north and east over the village, the Fabiánka settlement and the forested Tlustá hora ridge with its tall TV transmitter. A quick, rewarding overlook on the wooded hills south of the city.

name: jedním tahem (specialty café)
hook: Industrial-chic specialty café with its own bakery.
blurb: Set in the raw industrial fabric of the Baťa factory district, jedním tahem is Zlín's standout specialty coffee spot, pairing carefully sourced beans with an in-house bakery. The big, airy space draws students and locals for espresso, filter and cake. Listed on the European Coffee Trip guide.

name: Mr. Coffee (roastery)
hook: Roastery-café for grab-and-go specialty coffee.
blurb: A small Zlín roastery and café on Školní street right beside Park Komenského, selling specialty coffee drinks plus its own roasted beans and home-brewing gear. Little seating inside — built for taking your cup into the neighbouring park. A local specialty anchor on European Coffee Trip.

name: Happy Coffee
hook: Cosy well-rated café by náměstí Práce.
blurb: Right by the náměstí Práce transport hub in the heart of the Baťa civic centre, Happy Coffee is a warmly reviewed café serving a solid range of coffees, desserts and savoury snacks. Friendly baristas and a cosy interior — a reliable central pit stop before rolling on toward Otrokovice.
